Nordea Restructures Investment Banking Leadership Following Staff Exodus - Di
data.description:
Nordea is restructuring the leadership of its investment bank after a period marked by staff departures.

The division is now introducing a dual leadership structure, according to a report by Dagens industri. Lars Fischer will remain as co-head with responsibility for the debt market, while Stefan Löhr has been appointed co-head with responsibility for the equity market.

Löhr will also retain his current role as head of Private Equity and International Institutions.

Central African Republic court validates Touadera's re-election
data.description:
BANGUI, Jan 19 (Reuters) - Central African Republic's Constitutional Court on Monday validated President Faustin-Archange Touadera's victory in a disputed December 28 presidential election that handed him a third term.

Even before provisional results were announced on January 5, Touadera's opponents had alleged fraud but the government denied that.

"On behalf of the Central African people, the Constitutional Court declares Faustin Archange Touadera president-elect in the first round with 77.9% of the vote," President of the Constitutional Council Jean-Pierre Waboe said.

Touadera's main rival, former Prime Minister Anicet-Georges Dologuele, received 13.5% of the vote, final results from the electoral commission showed.

The Constitutional Court said it rejected an appeal that Dologuele had lodged after the announcement of provisional results.

Another competitor, former Prime Minister Henri-Marie Dondra who got 2.97% of the vote, last week acknowledged Touadera's victory and said he decided not to appeal.

"I made this decision to preserve peace and avoid reigniting tensions. I call on the president-elect to take the necessary measures to reconcile the Central African people and organize a genuine dialogue to ease political tensions in the country," Dondra told Reuters. 

Touadera, a 68-year-old mathematician who took power a decade ago, campaigned on his security record in the chronically unstable nation.

His victory will likely further the interests of Russia, which has traded security assistance for access to resources including gold and diamonds.

(Reporting by Pacome Pabandji; Writing by Anait Miridzhanian;Editing by Andrew Cawthorne)

TSX opens lower as Trump's Europe tariff threat rattles markets
data.description:
Jan 19 (Reuters) - Canada's main stock index opened lower on Monday as U.S. President Donald Trump's threat to impose tariffs on eight European countries rattled global markets, while domestic investors assessed key Canadian inflation data.

At 09:32 a.m. ET, the S&P/TSX Composite Index was down 0.1% at 32,991.06 points.

(Reporting by Ragini Mathur in BengaluruEditing by Tomasz Janowski)

EXECUTIVE CHANGES: Genflow Biosciences names Berdugo as new chair
data.description:
(Alliance News) - The following is a round-up of London-listed company director and manager changes announced on Monday and not separately reported by Alliance News:

----------

Amicorp FS (UK) PLC - fund services provider - Chief Financial Officer Tat Cheung 'Stephen' Wong on Monday announces his decision to step down, effective from January 29. Company says he is leaving to pursue other business opportunities. Announces that Senior Finance Manager Shane Gordon Anderson "will take on the financial reporting role". However, he "will not be joining the board at this time". Anderson was finance director "at a fintech company" before joining Amicorp, and head of finance "at a multi-national fund services group".

----------

Genflow Biosciences PLC - London-based biotechnology firm - Says on Monday that Gad Berdugo has joined the board, effective immediately, and will assume the role of independent non-executive chair. Tamara Joseph, the outgoing chair, will remain at Genflow as a director. Berdugo is currently the managing partner of biotechnology-focused strategic and financial advisory company Explorium Capital LLC, and his most recent roles include serving as chief business officer at Editas Medicine and Nutcracker Therapeutics. Genflow says he has a proven record of driving strategic partnerships, securing capital, and scaling both private and public development-stage biotechnology companies. Also highlights his "deep technical and market expertise in RNA-based therapeutics, including lipid nanoparticle (LNP) delivery, development, and manufacturing". Genflow also expects Berdugo's appointment to further strengthen its US presence and expand its global engagement capacity.

----------

Amala Foods PLC - cash shell - Appoints Simon Grant-Rennick as an executive director on Monday, effective immediately. Grant-Rennick is currently the executive chair at Evrima PLC, a non-executive director at Imara Gold PLC, and a non-executive at Everest Global PLC, alongside various other directorships. His previous directorships include being chair of Igraine PLC from 2021 to 2025, and of Elk Investments Ltd from 1998 to 2021. Amala says: "His expertise encompasses not only mining and minerals but also metals, agriculture and property." Notes that he has managed public and private mining firms in Uganda and Malawi, and metal trading ones in the UK and Bermuda. Company also says Aidan Bishop has resigned as a director, but "will continue to assist the board as required". Finally, Amala says it has been making progress towards identifying a suitable deal that could lead to a reverse takeover. Says it will update the market in due course.

----------

DeFi Development Corporation UK PLC - provider of AI platform that allows businesses to automate tasks, formerly named Cykel AI PLC - Announces on Thursday the appointments of Hadley Stern as non-executive chair, and Nathalie Maggi as executive director & chief financial officer, both effective from the day of the announcement. DeFi says Maggi was senior finance manager at cryptocurrency exchange Binance for four years, and "brings over a decade of international finance leadership across fintech and crypto, with deep experience navigating high-growth, regulated, and innovative markets." Stern, meanwhile, "is a veteran executive in digital assets and emerging technologies". He was formerly chief commercial officer at solana staking company Marinade Finance from mid-2024 to November last year, and was chief operating officer at blockchain infrastructure company Bloq Inc. He was also head of the Amazon Web Services Innovation Lab from 2020 to 2021.

----------

By Emma Curzon, Alliance News reporter

Comments and questions to newsroom@alliancenews.com

Copyright 2026 Alliance News Ltd. All Rights Reserved.

Air Transat to Launch New Loyalty Program With Desjardins, Visa
data.description:
By Adriano Marchese


Air Transat will launch a new loyalty program, teaming up with Desjardins Group and Visa, to offer co-branded credit cards and travel rewards.

The Montreal-based airline said Monday that the collaboration with Desjardins, a Canadian financial cooperative, is part of a strategy to offer travellers a loyalty program with exclusive benefits and a broader travel experience.

Desjardins will issue the cards which will run on Visa's payments network.

The companies didn't disclose details of the benefits or earning structure but said more information will come closer to launch.


Write to Adriano Marchese at adriano.marchese@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

01-19-26 0920ET

data.title: South African rand muted, investors await inflation data
data.description:
JOHANNESBURG, Jan 19 (Reuters) - South Africa's rand was subdued on Monday as risk appetite waned amid geopolitical tensions and traders looked ahead to domestic inflation data due this week for clues on the central bank's rate-cutting path.

At 1355 GMT the rand traded at 16.4175 against the dollar, little changed from Friday's closing level.

ETM Analytics said that risk appetite might be in shorter supply at the start of the week after U.S. President Donald Trump threatened a wave of increasing trade tariffs on key European allies till the U.S. is allowed to buy Greenland.

Like other risk-sensitive currencies, the rand often takes its cue from global drivers such as U.S. policy.

Domestically-focused traders will examine consumer inflation data on Wednesday, which will impact monetary policy and has implications for South Africa's financial markets.

"Consensus estimates have the inflation rate rising marginally to 3.6%, although the downside risk to this data is high. With the ZAR having performed well in recent months, it is likely the market has underestimated the ZAR's impact," ETM Analytics said in a research note.

On the Johannesburg Stock Exchange, the Top-40 index last traded flat.

South Africa's benchmark 2035 government bond weaker, with the yield up 7 basis points to 8.415%.

(Reporting by Anathi Madubela;Additional reporting by Sfundo Parakozov;Editing by Gareth Jones and Ros Russell)

data.title: Barrick Appoints Helen Cai as Chief Financial Officer
data.description:
By Robb M. Stewart


Barrick Mining named director Helen Cai as its next chief financial officer, succeeding Graham Shuttleworth, who has been in the role since the miner's acquisition of Randgold Resources seven years ago.

Cai will become senior executive vice president and finance chief on March 1, when Shuttleworth leaves Barrick following the release of its year-end financial results.

Barrick said Cai will work with Shuttleworth until his departure.

The change comes as the Canadian company considers spinning off its North American gold assets into a listed business, separating the operations from gold and copper mines in other parts of the world. It also comes after Barrick in late September unexpectedly parted ways with former Chief Executive Mark Bristow, who took the helm with the takeover of Randgold, an Africa-focused gold producer he founded.

Chief Operating Officer and interim CEO Mark Hill said Cai brings deep financial expertise and decades of mining sector experience that will be invaluable as Barrick focuses on driving improved performance and shareholder value.

Cai has served on the Barrick board since late 2021 and brings more than two decades of experience in equity research, corporate finance, strategic planning, capital markets, and mergers and acquisitions, primarily with Goldman Sachs and China International Capital.

Shuttleworth, who had been finance director and chief financial officer of Randgold, became Barrick's CFO at the start of 2019.

After launching a review of its operations that put greater emphasis on the North American assets, which account for more than half of its gold production, Barrick late last year said it was exploring an initial public offering of its North American operations. The new business would be anchored by the majority-owned Nevada Gold Mines and the Pueblo Viejo mine in the Dominican Republic, as well as its wholly-owned Fourmile discovery in Nevada.

If a breakup goes ahead, Barrick said it intends to retain a controlling majority interest in the new company, along with its various other assets globally.


Write to Robb M. Stewart at robb.stewart@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

01-19-26 0910ET

Oberoi Realty Limited Reports Earnings Results for the Third Quarter and Nine Months Ended December 31, 2025
data.description:
Oberoi Realty Limited reported earnings results for the third quarter and nine months ended December 31, 2025. For the third quarter, the company reported sales was INR 14,926.4 million compared to INR 14,110.8 million a year ago. Revenue was INR 15,617.4 million compared to INR 14,602.7 million a year ago. Net income was INR 6,226.4 million compared to INR 6,183.8 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was INR 17.6 compared to INR 17.01 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was INR 17.12 compared to INR 17.01 a year ago.
For the nine months, sales was INR 42,592.3 million compared to INR 41,361.3 million a year ago. Revenue was INR 44,805.6 million compared to INR 42,608.4 million a year ago. Net income was INR 18,041.5 million compared to INR 17,923.4 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was INR 50.09 compared to INR 49.29 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was INR 49.62 compared to INR 49.29 a year ago.

NYSE Plans Move Into Blockchain With Tokenized-Securities Trading Platform
data.description:
By Adriano Marchese


The New York Stock Exchange plans to launch a platform for trading tokenized securities, pushing the exchange into blockchain-based markets.

Intercontinental Exchange, parent company of the exchange, said Monday the new platform will allow for tokenized trading, which includes 24/7 operations, instant settlement while using stablecoins--which are fiat currency-backed digital tokens--for funding.

The NYSE said it will seek regulatory approval for a new venue to allow trading and instant settlement of tokenized shares as well as digital?only securities.

The company said the initiative is part of a broader push to modernize its clearing operations to better handle tokenized collateral and money transfers outside normal banking hours.

The company is working with banks including BNY and Citigroup on tokenized deposits that could move through its six clearinghouses.


Write to Adriano Marchese at adriano.marchese@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

01-19-26 0901ET
